purpose: Dated public-journal visual-language matrix for FigOps journal tracks.
claim_boundary: This matrix records official public source anchors and FigOps encoded tokens as of the matrix date.
It does not claim latest publisher compliance, complete journal coverage, or automatic publishability.
Journal-specific visual feel is captured only where evidence supports it; tracks are not forced to look different.

official_submission_constraints.source_name: Nature research figure guide: preparing figures - our specifications
constraints: Graph axes should be labelled with units and include axis lines and tick marks.
Figure graph text has a minimum size of 5 pt.
Standard sans-serif fonts such as Arial or Helvetica are requested.
Nature asks authors to avoid background gridlines, decorative effects, overlapping text, hard-to-read backgrounds, and reliance on colored text alone.
Figure text should remain editable and not be outlined.
One-column and two-column figure formats are required.
Nature Communications final image files use 88 mm single-column and 180 mm double-column width anchors.
Line art should be vector where possible; bitmapped line art fallback is 1200 dpi.
Photographic and bitmapped images should be TIFF at minimum 300 dpi and supplied close to page size.

encoded_figops_tokens.notes: Encoded tokens combine official anchors and FigOps defaults; they are not a standalone publisher-compliance claim.

observed_visual_language.item: Clean, sparse graph treatment with labelled axes, standard sans-serif text, no background gridlines, and no decorative effects.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official visual-language interpretation of public Nature guidance.

observed_visual_language.item: Accessibility-conscious color use and avoidance of red/green-only encodings.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official FigOps heuristic derived from public accessibility wording.

unsupported_or_deferred: No claim that the nature track covers every Nature-family journal or the latest Nature-family production rule.
No automatic validation of editable text, font embedding, image layers, or final publisher production decisions.

official_submission_constraints.source_name: Science AAAS instructions for preparing an initial manuscript
constraints: Printed figure widths are usually 5.7 cm, 12.1 cm, or 18.4 cm.
Science references figure-preparation guidance for initial manuscript figures.
The encoded FigOps line and height floors are treated as local safety defaults unless independently confirmed in a current official source.

encoded_figops_tokens.notes: Compact geometry is official-source anchored; several stroke and density values are FigOps visual defaults.

observed_visual_language.item: Compact, high-density figure canvas driven primarily by the small 57 mm single-column width.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official visual-language interpretation of official width constraints plus FigOps token scaling.

observed_visual_language.item: Reduced marker, error-cap, and jitter sizes to keep dense plots legible at small final width.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official FigOps heuristic encoded in style tokens.

unsupported_or_deferred: No claim of full AAAS production compliance or latest Science-family rule coverage.
No automatic manuscript-embedded figure review, resolution audit, or publisher upload validation.

official_submission_constraints.source_name: ACS author guidelines: Appendix 2, Preparing Graphics
constraints: Single-column graphics can be up to 240 pt wide.
Double-column graphics are between 300 pt and 504 pt wide.
Maximum graphics depth is 660 pt including caption allowance.
Lettering should be no smaller than 4.5 pt.
Helvetica or Arial work well for lettering.
Lines should be no thinner than 0.5 pt.
ACS asks authors to avoid relying on color alone and to use symbols, labels, or patterns where needed for accessibility.

encoded_figops_tokens.notes: ACS hard constraints are official-source anchored; marker and density choices are FigOps heuristics.

observed_visual_language.item: Chemistry-oriented, accessible graphics with clear sans-serif lettering and non-color-only encodings.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official visual-language interpretation of ACS graphics and accessibility guidance.

observed_visual_language.item: Moderate line and marker scale for chemical, spectral, and materials plots near 85 mm width.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official FigOps heuristic encoded in style tokens.

unsupported_or_deferred: No automatic TOC graphic, cover art, image-manipulation, or journal-specific ACS title variant validation.
No claim that viridis is an ACS-mandated palette.

official_submission_constraints.source_name: Royal Society of Chemistry author guidelines for RSC Advances
constraints: Figures, schemes, and charts should be supplied as TIFF files at 600 dpi or greater; EPS or PDF can be supplied instead.
Images should fit within 8.3 cm single-column or 17.1 cm double-column width.
Images must be no longer than 23.3 cm and not larger than a single page.
Text, numerical data, and scale bars should be clearly legible.
Chemical structures use Arial/Helvetica 7 pt captions or atom labels and a 0.5 pt bond-width setting.

encoded_figops_tokens.notes: RSC width, height, and text anchors are official-source aligned; marker and distribution settings are FigOps heuristics.

observed_visual_language.item: Chemistry-publishing visual language: clearly legible labels, scale bars, and compact column-aware artwork.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official visual-language interpretation of RSC figure and chemical-structure guidance.

observed_visual_language.item: Higher tick/text floor than most other tracks, reflected by 7 pt minimum text and tick choices.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official FigOps interpretation of official legibility and chemical-structure text anchors.

unsupported_or_deferred: No automatic TOC graphic review, AI-figure licensing check, or RSC journal-specific exception handling.
No claim that general graph markers are official RSC chemical-structure settings.

official_submission_constraints.source_name: Elsevier artwork and media instructions: artwork sizing
constraints: Elsevier asks authors to check the journal-specific guide for authors because some publications have special instructions.
Normal text should generally finish at 7 pt and subscripts or superscripts no smaller than 6 pt; this is a rule of thumb rather than a strict rule.
Bitmap targets include 300 dpi halftone, 500 dpi combination art, and 1000 dpi line art.
Target sizes listed include 90 mm single column, 140 mm 1.5 column, and 190 mm double column.

encoded_figops_tokens.notes: Elsevier width and normal-text size anchors are official-source aligned; line floor and max height are conservative FigOps defaults.

observed_visual_language.item: Broad, uniform artwork style with larger single-column canvas and readable normal text.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official visual-language interpretation of Elsevier artwork sizing guidance.

observed_visual_language.item: Slightly larger markers and caps than compact tracks to use the 90 mm single-column canvas.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official FigOps heuristic encoded in style tokens.

unsupported_or_deferred: No claim that one Elsevier track satisfies every Elsevier journal-specific guide for authors.
No automatic AQC, pixel-count, file-format, or upload validation.

official_submission_constraints.source_name: Wiley author resources: guidelines for preparing figures
constraints: Figures should use preferred file types and original files where possible.
All words and symbols should be large enough for easy reading.
Individual figure files should be less than 10 MB.
Figures should be created at a size between 80 mm and 180 mm in width and between 300 dpi and 600 dpi.
Line art is preferably PDF and 600 dpi; images are preferably PNG or TIFF and 300 dpi.

encoded_figops_tokens.notes: Wiley official source gives broad size and resolution ranges; exact 85/178 mm tokens are FigOps track anchors, not a universal Wiley compliance claim.

observed_visual_language.item: Quality-first figure handling with broad accepted width range and emphasis on readable words and symbols.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official visual-language interpretation of Wiley figure-preparation guidance.

observed_visual_language.item: Near-default journal scale with slightly larger markers than ACS/RSC and compact error styling.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official FigOps heuristic encoded in style tokens.

unsupported_or_deferred: No claim that exact 85/178 mm geometry is an official global Wiley rule.
No automatic file-size, file-type, resolution, or journal-title-specific Wiley validation.

official_submission_constraints.access_note: Generic fetch was challenged by Cloudflare on 2026-07-04; this matrix records the official publisher URL and the repository's existing source anchor without claiming latest revalidation.
constraints: FigOps currently anchors Cell Press widths at 85 mm single column, 114 mm one-and-a-half column, and 174 mm full width.
FigOps currently anchors Cell Press minimum type size at 6 pt, minimum line weight at 0.5 pt, and maximum figure height at 200 mm.
Because the official URL was fetch-challenged in this session, these values are retained as encoded-source anchors rather than promoted to a latest-rule claim.

encoded_figops_tokens.notes: Cell Press source URL is recorded, but this session could not re-fetch it past Cloudflare; encoded tokens remain local FigOps anchors.

observed_visual_language.item: Biomedical journal canvas with 85/114/174 mm width slots and moderate 6 pt text floor.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official visual-language interpretation of existing FigOps source anchors.

observed_visual_language.item: Moderate marker and line scale similar to ACS/Wiley, without forcing a distinct decorative style.
observed_visual_language.basis: Non-official FigOps heuristic encoded in style tokens.

unsupported_or_deferred: No claim of latest Cell Press compliance because the official page was Cloudflare-challenged in this session.
No automatic validation of image mode, embedded fonts, panel labels, scale bars, or journal-specific Cell Press variants.
